title: World Geography Tour
description: Travel through countries, rivers, cities, landmarks, and maps.

questions.hint: It is strongly associated with ancient Egypt.
questions.explanation: The Nile River flows through Cairo and has shaped Egyptian history for millennia.

questions.prompt: Which country is both a continent and a country?
questions.hint: It is surrounded by the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
questions.explanation: Australia is commonly described as both a country and a continent.

questions.prompt: The imaginary line at 0 degrees latitude is the _____.
questions.hint: It divides Earth into Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
questions.explanation: The equator is the line of 0 degrees latitude.

questions.prompt: Which mountain range separates much of Europe from Asia?
questions.hint: The range runs north-south through western Russia.
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains are commonly used as part of the boundary between Europe and Asia.

questions.hint: Its major islands include Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.
questions.explanation: Japan is an archipelago made up of thousands of islands.

questions.prompt: Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.hint: It stretches across much of North Africa.
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert.

questions.prompt: Which city is home to the Colosseum?
questions.hint: It was the heart of the ancient Roman Empire.
questions.explanation: The Colosseum is in Rome, Italy.

questions.prompt: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
questions.hint: Its Arctic islands add a huge amount of shoreline.
questions.explanation: Canada has the world's longest coastline.

questions.prompt: The Andes are located in South America.
questions.hint: They run along the western edge of the continent.
questions.explanation: The Andes are a major mountain range along South America's western side.

questions.prompt: Which sea lies between Europe and Africa?
questions.hint: Ancient Greek, Roman, Egyptian, and Phoenician trade crossed it.
questions.explanation: The Mediterranean Sea lies between southern Europe and northern Africa.
